Patch Set 4:

(16 comments)

Rather than:

 #define OLEG_BUF_SIZE 100
 ...
 char buf[OLEG_BUF_SIZE];
 ...
 if (count >= OLEG_BUF_SIZE)
     ...

please do:

 char buf[128];
 ...
 if (count >= sizeof(buf))
     ...

Also if you would add the missing __user attributes as you go along then we can move (slowly) towards having these errors detected at build time rather than test time---which would save valuable resources.

(5 comments)

Patch Set 4:

(27 comments)

Thanks a lot for very helpful reviews, everyone.

I got everything nailed now I think and also fixed some new violators that crept in while I was getting back to this, which shows we really need to get this in ASAP to avoid future regressions.

Patch Set 11: Verified-1

Apparently current patch breaks sanity test 160a and 161c for some not yet clear reason.
Likely those tests assume some preexistign state thta is reset by the previous writes to all proc and lnet files.
Uploaded patch set 12.

Patch Set 12:

the only change this time is to exclude changelog_mask from list of files touched, as otherwise changelog-related tests don't feel good.

In general we need to update cfs_str2mask not to reset everything to min mask if all we feed to it is only garbage/empty string

Looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-4862 doc: Update lctl man page for LFSCK phase 2

commit.subject: LU-4563 Fix unsafe userspace access in many proc files
commit.message:
LU-4563 Fix unsafe userspace access in many proc files

Also add a test to make sure no new ones are added in the future

Change-Id: I2fe7d2cea5daef21f4279ce813ca35f6d91f68dc
Signed-off-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/9059
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
